name: Running Montgosso Circular Itinerary — Alto Pirineo Natural Park
name_original: Running Itinerario de Montgosso Circular — Parque Natural del Alto Pirineo
region_intro: Jogging routes around Alt Àneu traverse the heart of the Catalan Pyrenees, characterized by dramatic alpine peaks, pristine glacial lakes, and dense forests. The region encompasses significant portions of the Alt Pirineu Natural Park and is near the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, offering untouched natural environments. Runners can explore high-mountain lakes, extensive fir and pine forests, and lush valleys shaped by rivers, providing varied terrain for all abilities. Best jogging routes around Alt Àneu The most popular jogging route is Running Lago de Gerber from la Peülla — Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This difficult route leads to the glacial Lake Gerber within the national park. Another top favourite among local runners is Running Gerber Waterfall — Aigüestortes y Lago de San Mauricio National Park, a moderate 3.9 miles (6.2 km) path. This route offers views of the impressive Gerber Waterfall. Local runners also love the Running Montgosso Circular Itinerary — Alto Pirineo Natural Park, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through the Alt Pirineu Natural Park, often completed in about 50 minutes. Jogging around Alt Àneu is defined by high-mountain lakes, dense forests, and river valleys. The extensive network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging mountain runs. The routes in Alt Àneu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 5 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Alt Àneu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes can I find in Alt Àneu?
A: Alt Àneu offers a diverse range of running routes, from moderate paths to more challenging mountain runs. You'll find trails traversing dramatic alpine peaks, pristine glacial lakes, and dense forests within the &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Alt_Pirineu_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt Pirineu Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and near the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park. The region's extensive network of ancient paths and trails caters to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls in Alt Àneu?
A: Yes, Alt Àneu boasts several scenic running routes that include waterfalls. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939633785/running-gerber-waterfall-aigueestortes-y-lago-de-san-mauricio-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running Gerber Waterfall — Aigüestortes y Lago de San Mauricio National Park&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.9-mile (6.2 km) path offering views of the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3217857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gerber Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1033042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ratera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; along other trails in the area.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Alt Àneu?
A: Yes, there are circular running routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939652728/running-montgosso-circular-itinerary-alto-pirineo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running Montgosso Circular Itinerary — Alto Pirineo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.2-mile (6.8 km) trail that takes about 50 minutes to complete. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Isil&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.1 km long and also considered moderate.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while running in Alt Àneu?
A: Runners in Alt Àneu can enjoy breathtaking natural attractions. Many routes lead past stunning high-mountain lakes of glacial origin, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Sant Maurici&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/197506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ratera Lake&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3302745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Major of Colomers&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter extensive forests of fir and pine, lush river valleys, and dramatic Pyrenean peaks.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners in Alt Àneu?
A: Absolutely. Alt Àneu offers several challenging routes for experienced runners. One notable difficult trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918367721/running-lago-de-gerber-from-la-peuella-aigueestortes-i-estany-de-sant-maurici-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running Lago de Gerber from la Peülla — Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.1-mile (6.6 km) route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931079395/running-route-through-the-cabanes-valley-aigueestortes-national-park-and-lake-of-san-mauricio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running - Route through the Cabanes Valley&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 13 km long and features substantial elevation changes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Alt Àneu?
A: The running routes in Alt Àneu are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 5 reviews. Over 800 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural beauty, the diversity of landscapes, and the well-maintained trails that offer both serene forest runs and challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Is it necessary to obtain permits for running in the national parks around Alt Àneu?
A: While general access for jogging on established trails in Alt Pirineu Natural Park is usually unrestricted, specific regulations may apply for certain areas or activities within Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, especially during peak seasons or for overnight stays. It's always advisable to check the official websites of both parks for the most current information regarding permits or access restrictions before your visit.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Alt Àneu?
A: The best time for running in Alt Àneu is generally from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the trails are more accessible. Spring brings vibrant colors, while autumn offers stunning foliage. While some lower-altitude paths might be runnable in winter, many higher-altitude trails will be covered in snow, making them suitable for snowshoeing or skiing rather than running.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Alt Àneu?
A: Alt Àneu offers a range of trails, and while specific 'easy' routes for families are not explicitly highlighted in the guide data, the region's extensive network includes paths of varying difficulty. Many trails along lakeshores or through valleys are less strenuous and can be enjoyed by families. It's recommended to check the difficulty ratings and distances of individual routes on komoot to find the best fit for your family.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Alt Àneu?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on many trails in the Alt Àneu region, especially within the &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Alt_Pirineu_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt Pirineu Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, often requiring them to be on a leash. However, regulations can be stricter within the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, where access for dogs might be limited to certain areas or prohibited to protect wildlife. Always check specific park rules and local signage before heading out with your dog.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Alt Àneu?
A: Parking facilities are typically available near trailheads, especially in and around the main villages like Esterri d'Àneu, Espot, and La Guingueta d'Àneu, which serve as gateways to the parks. For routes within the national parks, designated parking areas are usually provided at access points like La Peülla for Aigüestortes. It's advisable to research specific trailheads on komoot or local tourist information for precise parking locations.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access running routes in Alt Àneu?
A: Public transport options in Alt Àneu are more limited compared to larger urban areas, but some services connect the main villages. During peak tourist seasons, shuttle services might operate to popular trailheads within the national parks, such as to the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park from Espot. For detailed and up-to-date information, it's best to consult local transport schedules or tourist information centers in the region.
